feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Google planning to release millions of mosquitoes into California to help stop diseases
entry.summary: California could soon become a testing ground for one of Google’s most ambitious public health projects yet. The tech giant is seeking federal approval to release up to 32 million specially t…

entry.title: US adult cigarette smoking rate hits another all-time low
entry.summary: The cigarette smoking rate among U.S. adults dropped to another all-time low last year

entry.title: I Survived Breast Cancer. I Wasn't Prepared for What Came Next.
entry.summary: I just hit my 5-year breast cancer remission anniversary. These are the 4 hard things no one told me about being a survivor.

entry.title: Tracking the 2026 Ebola outbreak in maps and figures: Locations, severity, how contagious it is and more
entry.summary: Learn more about the deadly 2026 Ebola outbreak, including how many cases there are so far, which countries are most affected, how this outbreak compares with others in severity and more.

entry.title: Rabid bat found in Snohomish County
entry.summary: In Snohomish County, six bats have tested positive over the last decade, but this week's bat is the second to test positive for rabies in Washington this year.

entry.title: There’s a Deadly ‘Rotating’ Soup of Forbidden Toxins at Lake Erie
entry.summary: Starting in early spring, Lake Erie's algae situation gets so toxic that officials make sure to monitor it—but it seems far from enough.

entry.title: A ‘Black Hole’ in Your Consciousness Makes You See a Unique Version of Reality, Neuroscientists Say
entry.summary: Turns out we’re not all watching the same version of the world unfold, just continuously updating our own personal “simulation.”

entry.title: Low Folate and B12 Proven to Drive Chronic Fatigue
entry.summary: A new study links elevated homocysteine from B12 and folate deficits to physical fatigue in men and low motivation in women.

entry.title: 4 Things a Cardiologist Does Before Bed for Better Sleep
entry.summary: A cardiologist shares four nighttime habits that help improve sleep quality and duration.

entry.title: The High-Protein Mediterranean Diet Casserole I Can’t Stop Making
entry.summary: Creamy Chicken Florentine Casserole is packed with protein, whole grains and Mediterranean-inspired ingredients—making it a comforting, nourishing dinner you’ll want on repeat.

entry.title: Drug that grew extra teeth in mice enters first human trial
entry.summary: Researchers at Kyoto University Hospital have begun the first human trial of TRG-035, a drug meant to grow new teeth in people who are missing them. Humans keep a set…

entry.title: 3 receiving preventive treatment after exposure to rabid bat in Snohomish County home
entry.summary: Three Snohomish County residents are receiving preventive treatment for rabies after being exposed to a bat that tested positive for the virus, county health of

entry.title: 911 dispatchers rally around 4-year-old Rigby boy after brain tumor diagnosis
entry.summary: RIGBY — A group of 911 dispatchers is supporting a young boy from Rigby and his family after he was recently diagnosed with cancer. His name is Stetson Poole, and his mother, MeKenna Poole, is a Jefferson County 911 dispatcher. She has been working as a dispa…

entry.title: Kohl's employee with measles may have infected others at East Lampeter Township store [Update]
entry.summary: A Kohl’s department store employee infected with measles may have exposed others to the disease while at the 2350 Lincoln Highway East store in East Lampeter Township May 21, 23,

entry.title: Two Distinct Autism Subtypes Identified Via Brain Connectivity
entry.summary: A new study uses fMRI and mouse models to identify synaptic hypoconnectivity and immune hyperconnectivity autism subtypes.

entry.title: Think You’re Immune to Late-Day Caffeine? Your Sleep May Disagree
entry.summary: Recent research finds that caffeine can reduce the time we spend in deep sleep, even if it goes unnoticed.

entry.title: How To Get Rid of Fatty Liver Disease and Lose Weight Faster After 50
entry.summary: If you feel like the number on the scale keeps creeping up and your energy is dragging, your liver may be waving a white flag. Nearly a quarter of Americans...

entry.title: Class action lawsuit filed against Broome County and Sheriff Fred Akshar after salmonella outbreak at county jail
entry.summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Broome County and Broome County Sheriff Fred Akshar after a massive Salmonella outbreak at the Broome County Jail.

entry.title: Here's the No. 1 breakfast mistake Americans make every morning, says oncologist and longevity expert
entry.summary: Ezekiel J. Emanuel has spent 35 years as an oncologist and expert on health policy. He shares his breakfast tips for better metabolic health, energy and longevity.

entry.title: Scientists Discover Surprising Anti-Aging Power Hidden in Aged Garlic
entry.summary: A compound in aged garlic extract may help reduce age-related muscle decline by activating anti-aging pathways connecting fat tissue, the brain, and muscles. Can a compound found in a common kitchen ingredient help protect muscle health as people age? New res…

entry.title: Gut Bacteria Have Been Throwing Off Calorie Counts For 130 Years: Scientists May Have A Fix
entry.summary: Gut microbes may be skewing calorie counts. Scientists built a new model that outperforms the 130-year-old Atwater formula.

entry.title: 8 High-Protein Snacks That Aren’t Cottage Cheese
entry.summary: Looking for snacks with more protein than cottage cheese? Learn 8 high-protein options, from Greek yogurt and eggs to edamame and tuna.

entry.title: What Happens to Your Blood Sugar When You Drink a Smoothie Every Morning
entry.summary: Smoothies raise blood sugar, but adding fat, protein and fiber helps prevent spikes. Dietitians share how to assemble a blood sugar-friendly smoothie.

entry.title: A silent kidney crisis is spreading far faster than experts expected
entry.summary: A sweeping global study found that chronic kidney disease now affects nearly 800 million people and has become one of the world's leading causes of death. Often silent in its early stages, the condition is also a major contributor to heart disease and ma…

entry.title: 5 Processed Foods Dietitians Actually Want You to Eat More Of
entry.summary: Nutrition experts say that how food is processed and what's added during processing is more important than whether the food is processed. Frozen produce, yogurt or kefir, spreads and dips, and canned proteins are just a few processed foods they recommend eati…

entry.title: 6 habits a happiness expert uses to boost her mood, even on busy days
entry.summary: Sonja Lyubomirsky has spent decades studying the science of happiness. Here are the daily strategies she finds most effective.

entry.title: WHO chief lands in Congo to address rare Ebola outbreak amid distrust and insecurity
entry.summary: The head of the World Health Organization arrived in Congo's capital, Kinshasa, to witness efforts against an outbreak of a rare type of Ebola virus.

entry.title: Can Ozempic Treat Alcoholism? Here’s What You Should Know
entry.summary: A recent trial is the latest to suggest that GLP-1s aren't just for treating diabetes and obesity.

entry.title: Molecular Glue Is Shaping Up to Be the Next Billion-Dollar Cancer Breakthrough
entry.summary: Revolution Medicines’ success in pancreatic cancer has ignited interest in a new type of medicine that's sending dealmaking into overdrive.

entry.title: Three new measles cases in metro Atlanta confirmed by state
entry.summary: Officials with the Georgia Department of Public Health say the family traveled internationally and was unvaccinated.

entry.title: Ebola and hantavirus outbreaks raise questions about Trump's health agency cuts
entry.summary: Infectious disease specialists say the viruses are unlikely to become pandemics, but some are still raising concerns about the federal health response.

entry.title: Vomiting virus is spreading across most of the U.S.
entry.summary: Flu season may be mostly behind us, but levels of norovirus, the nasty, highly contagious stomach bug, are rising, according to wastewater data.

entry.title: Vomiting virus is spreading across most of the U.S.
entry.summary: Flu season may be mostly behind us, but levels of norovirus, the nasty, highly contagious stomach bug, are rising, according to wastewater data.

entry.title: Just Minutes of Intense Exercise Could Slash Disease Risk by 50%
entry.summary: A few breathless minutes of exercise each week could have a far bigger impact on your health than most people realize.

entry.title: Hantavirus-exposed cruise passengers may soon be allowed to return home but must remain under 24/7 watch
entry.summary: The US government is prepared to allow American passengers who were exposed to a unique strain of hantavirus to return home as early as Monday, provided their states post a monitor outside their homes 24/7 for the remaining three weeks of their six-week quara…

entry.title: Why More People in Their 30s Are Suddenly Getting Colon Cancer
entry.summary: A major Swiss study found that colorectal cancer is becoming increasingly common in adults under 50, even as rates decline in older age groups.

entry.title: Study reveals nearly 40% of cancers may be preventable; doctor explains how everyday habits are raising the risk
entry.summary: Cancer is often spoken about in whispers. For years, many people believed it was mostly a matter of bad luck, genetics, or ageing. But growing scientific evidence is changing that belief. A study published in Nature Medicine found that nearly 40% of cancer ca…

entry.title: Experts convened by WHO advise on candidate treatments and vaccines for Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus
entry.summary: In response to the current outbreak of Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus occurring in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, with cases also reported in Uganda, WHO convened several of its expert and advisory groups. These groups assessed potential v…

entry.title: 5 Habits to Start Now for Healthier Aging, According to Oxford Scientists
entry.summary: Oxford scientists reveal 5 habits for healthy aging: sleep, mindset, exercise, diet and stress. Learn the SMEDs acronym and practical wellness tips.

entry.title: College Student Adjusts Schedule After Roommate Complains About Her Going Out at Night
entry.summary: The best of the internet, sifted, and sorted.

entry.title: Infectious Information 5/28/2026
entry.summary: The health authorities are “playing catch-up” on the burgeoning Ebola outbreak, made worse by violent conflict in the region. US and global dengue cases are surging. A new case in Spain brings the…

entry.title: Apple Watch sleep data helps Harvard researchers study menopause transition
entry.summary: Researchers at Harvard analyzed more than 94,000 nights of Apple Watch sleep data to better understand how sleep patterns change during perimenopause.

entry.title: First-of-its-kind UCSF study shows blood test may catch early cognitive trouble
entry.summary: Alzheimer's disease remains a top cause of death in the U.S. and cases are expected to almost double by 2060.

entry.title: Ozempic may be reshaping the brain, scientists say
entry.summary: Scientists are exploring how GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic may not only be a metabolism breakthrough, but could also reshape some patients’ brains. GLP-1s were originally developed to treat diabetes and obesity, but researchers at the University of Colorado Anschu…

entry.title: Gene therapies to fix failing hearts gain steam after years in the doldrums
entry.summary: Treatments aim to bulk up failing hearts to restore their vigour — but can the field move on from past controversy?

entry.title: Steel-Cut Oats vs. Greek Yogurt: Which Is the More Balanced Breakfast?
entry.summary: Both steel-cut oats and Greek yogurt offer a variety of health benefits, but Greek yogurt has more protein and is likely to keep you feeling fuller.

entry.title: 'I wish I knew’: Billings skin cancer patient warns of harmful sun exposure
entry.summary: A Billings skin cancer patient is warning people of the harmful effects of sun exposure, which he believes led to his cancer in 2008.

entry.title: In 1938 the average American spent 47 minutes a day doing nothing — by 2026 that number had almost vanished, and researchers say that lost time was never idle, it was when the brain did its most important work
entry.summary: In 1965, the earliest large-scale time-diary study ever conducted found that the average American between the ages of 25 and 35 spent the equivalent of nearly an hour each day doing nothing in particular — not reading, not socialising, not listening to anythi…

entry.title: Skip the Protein Bars—Dietitians Recommend These 9 High-Protein, High-Fiber Snacks Instead
entry.summary: We asked dietitians for the best protein- and fiber-packed snacks that aren't a boring protein bar. Here are the delicious and nutritious snacks they recommend.

entry.title: Health department issues warning after rabid bat found inside home, resident exposed in McHenry County
entry.summary: Health officials said that a resident was exposed to a bat, which tested positive for rabies, that was found in a house in McHenry County, the first rabid bat discovery in the county this year.
